anti_claim: This fixture validates public synthetic indirect prompt-injection information-flow replay metadata only; it does not use real email, docs, browser snippets, accounts, raw prompts, credentials, provider payloads, live tools, benchmark claims, source mutation, or release.

anti_claim_contract.rule: Prompt-injection flow material must state that synthetic replay receipts are not live robustness, account, prompt, provider, benchmark, source-mutation, or release authority

omission_receipt.reason: public slice carries synthetic trust/taint/flow metadata and redacted receipt evidence only

forbidden_public_inputs: real email, document, browser, or account material

receipt_expectations: receipt must cite source refs, source pattern ids, source/flow/verdict/output/replay counts, negative-case coverage, private-state scan, authority ceiling, and anti-claim

source_coupling_contract.rule: macro source refs stay path-level metadata and public replacements stay public-root relative

standard_payload.anti_purpose: Treating final-answer behavior, a demo transcript, or untrusted tool text as evidence that prompt injection is solved.
standard_payload.ontology: A public-safe indirect prompt-injection information-flow replay: synthetic trust labels, taint graph, policy verdicts, sanitized outputs, cold replay, falsification fixtures, and authority ceilings.
standard_payload.teleology: Make Microcosm show how prompt-injection robustness-adjacent claims are admitted only when source trust, taint, policy, sanitization, negative cases, receipts, and ceilings align.

validation_rules: require synthetic source documents with trust labels, taint labels, and no raw body export
require source-to-sink information-flow graph rows before policy verdicts
require allow, warn, block, and review verdicts to precede synthetic actions
require sanitized outputs to disclose no trusted context and obey no untrusted instruction

reject real accounts, secret exfiltration, raw prompt body export, tool-output instruction authority, hidden-system-message promotion, credential exfiltration, final-answer-only success, and ungated untrusted privileged sinks
emit redacted receipts and private-state scan
